content.problemStatement:
A competition with $N$ participants (numbered $1$ through $N$) is taking place in Chefland. There are ${N−1}$ rounds in the competition; in each round, two arbitrarily chosen contestants battle, one of them loses and drops out of the competition. There are 10 types of weapons (numbered 1 through 10). You are given $N$ binary strings $1, 2, …, S $, each strings contains information about the weapons held by the contestant. If the character at an index is '1', the contestant has that type of weapon, otherwise the contestant doesn't have that weapon. During each battle, if the contestants have same type of weapon, that type of weapon is destroyed; after the battle, the winner collects all remaining (not destroyed) weapons of the loser. Note that each contestant may win or lose regardless of the weapons he/she has.

For Example: 
If the two contestant have the following strings  
0101010111  
1011011010  
the winner will have the weapons  
1110001101

Which operator do these fights resemble?
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ — denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
